---
title: "Mini_Age_Performance_Ex"
output: pdf_document
date: "2025-09-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
summary(cars)
```

Lahman dataset to fit 

```{r}
library(Lahman)
library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
Batting <- Lahman::Batting
People <- Lahman::People
# ---- Build dataset ----
hit <- Batting %>%
  filter(lgID %in% c("AL","NL")) %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay),
            by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear,
                           pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm=TRUE),
                           pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm=TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 42)) %>%
  mutate(
    SLG = (H + X2B + 2*X3B + 3*HR) / pmax(AB,1),
    ISO = SLG - H / pmax(AB,1),
    playerID = factor(playerID)   # ensure factor here
  )

# ---- take a smaller sample of players ----
set.seed(123)
n_players <- length(unique(hit$playerID))
sample_size <- min(200, n_players)   # reduced to 200 for speed
sampled_ids <- sample(unique(hit$playerID), sample_size)

hit_sub <- hit %>%
  filter(playerID %in% sampled_ids)

# ---- Fit GAM age curve with random player effect ----
m <- gam(ISO ~ s(age, k=10) + s(playerID, bs="re"),
         data=hit_sub, method="REML", weights=PA)

# ---- Predict curve ----
newdat <- data.frame(age = seq(18, 42, by=0.25),
                     playerID = NA)
pred <- predict(m, newdat, se.fit=TRUE)
curve_df <- data.frame(
  age=newdat$age,
  ISO=pred$fit,
  lo=pred$fit - 2*pred$se.fit,
  hi=pred$fit + 2*pred$se.fit
)

# ---- Plot ----
ggplot(curve_df, aes(x=age, y=ISO)) +
  geom_line(color="blue", size=1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=lo, ymax=hi), fill="blue", alpha=0.2) +
  labs(title="Mini Age-Performance Curve (ISO, 200 Sampled Players)",
       x="Age", y="Isolated Power (ISO)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14)


#MINI DATASET EX ISO SLUG\


set.seed(123) # reproducible sampling

# Build smaller dataset: pick 200 random players
players_sample <- sample(unique(Batting$playerID), 200)

hit <- Batting %>%
  filter(playerID %in% players_sample, lgID %in% c("AL","NL")) %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay),
            by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear, pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm=TRUE), pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm=TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 42)) %>%
  mutate(
    SLG = (H + X2B + 2*X3B + 3*HR)/pmax(AB,1),
    ISO = SLG - H/pmax(AB,1)
  )
# 🔹 Add sampling HERE
# set.seed(123)  # reproducibility
# sampled_ids <- sample(unique(hit$playerID), 200)   # pick 500 players
# hit_sub <- hit %>% filter(playerID %in% sampled_ids)

# Fit GAM age curve with random player effect
m <- gam(ISO ~ s(age, k=10) + s(playerID, bs="re"),
         data=hit_sub, method="REML", weights=PA)

# Predict curve
newdat <- data.frame(age = seq(18, 42, by=0.25), playerID = NA)
pred <- predict(m, newdat, se.fit=TRUE)
curve_df <- data.frame(age=newdat$age, ISO=pred$fit,
                       lo=pred$fit-2*pred$se.fit, hi=pred$fit+2*pred$se.fit)

# Plot
ggplot(curve_df, aes(x=age, y=ISO)) +
  geom_line(color="blue", size=1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=lo, ymax=hi), fill="blue", alpha=0.2) +
  labs(title="Mini Age-Performance Curve (ISO, sample of 500 players)",
       x="Age", y="Isolated Power (ISO)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14)

```

wOBA EX\

```{r}
library(Lahman); library(dplyr); library(mgcv); library(ggplot2); library(lubridate)

set.seed(123) # reproducible sampling

# Build smaller dataset: pick 200 random players
players_sample <- sample(unique(Batting$playerID), 200)
hit <- Batting %>%
  filter(playerID %in% players_sample, lgID %in% c("AL","NL")) %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay),
            by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear, pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm=TRUE), pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm=TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 42)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Compute wOBA using simplified 2023 MLB weights (adjust if needed)
    wOBA = (0.69*BB + 0.72*HBP + 0.89*H + 1.27*X2B + 1.62*X3B + 2.10*HR) / PA
  )

# Fit GAM on reduced dataset (global curve only)
m <- gam(wOBA ~ s(age, k=8), data=hit, method="REML", weights=PA)

# Predict curve
newdat <- data.frame(age = seq(18, 42, by=0.25))
pred <- predict(m, newdat, se.fit=TRUE)
curve_df <- data.frame(age=newdat$age, wOBA=pred$fit,
                       lo=pred$fit-2*pred$se.fit, hi=pred$fit+2*pred$se.fit)

# Plot
ggplot(curve_df, aes(x=age, y=wOBA)) +
  geom_line(color="blue", size=1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=lo, ymax=hi), fill="blue", alpha=0.2) +
  labs(title="Mini Age-Performance Curve (wOBA, sample of players)",
       x="Age", y="wOBA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14)

```
```{r}
download.file("https://www.baseball-reference.com/data/war_daily_bat.txt", destfile = "war_daily_bat.csv")
war_data <- read.csv("war_daily_bat.csv")
str(war_data)

```


MINI WAR EX:\

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)

# Convert WAR to numeric
war_data <- war_data %>%
  mutate(WAR = as.numeric(WAR))

# Merge with Lahman Batting data
hit <- Batting %>%
  filter(#playerID %in% sample(unique(Batting$playerID), 200),
         lgID %in% c("AL","NL")) %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay),
            by = "playerID") %>%
  left_join(war_data %>% select(player_ID, year_ID, team_ID, WAR),
            by = c("playerID" = "player_ID", "yearID" = "year_ID", "teamID" = "team_ID")) %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear, pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm = TRUE), pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm = TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 42))

# Fit GAM on reduced dataset
m <- gam(WAR ~ s(age, k = 8), data = hit, method = "REML", weights = PA)

# Predict curve
newdat <- data.frame(age = seq(18, 42, by = 0.25))
pred <- predict(m, newdat, se.fit = TRUE)
curve_df <- data.frame(age = newdat$age, WAR = pred$fit,
                       lo = pred$fit - 2 * pred$se.fit, hi = pred$fit + 2 * pred$se.fit)

# Plot
ggplot(curve_df, aes(x = age, y = WAR)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "Mini Age-Performance Curve (WAR, sample of players)",
       x = "Age", y = "WAR") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)


```

```{r}
m <- gam(WAR ~ s(age, k = 8) + s(age, playerID, bs="fs",m=1, k=8), data = hit%>%mutate(playerID=factor(playerID)), method = "REML", weights = PA)
plot(m)


```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# take a random sample of 50 players
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
sample_ids <- sample(unique(hit$playerID), 50)

hit_sample <- hit %>% 
  filter(playerID %in% sample_ids)

# plot smoothed career arcs
ggplot(hit_sample, aes(x = age, y = WAR, group = playerID, color = playerID)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess", span = 0.6, size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Sampled Player Seasons: WAR by Age",
    x = "Age",
    y = "WAR"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


```


```{r}
library(Lahman)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Player-season dataset
batting_data <- Batting %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay), by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear, pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm=TRUE), pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm=TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF,
    wOBA = (0.69*BB + 0.72*HBP + 0.89*H + 1.27*X2B + 1.62*X3B + 2.10*HR) / pmax(PA,1),
    BA = H / pmax(AB,1)
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 45))


#INDIVIDUAL PLAYER STATS
set.seed(123)  # reproducibility

# Find players with at least 10 eligible seasons
long_career_players <- batting_data %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  summarise(seasons = n(), .groups="drop") %>%
  filter(seasons >= 10)

# Sample 8 players at random
sample_players <- sample(long_career_players$playerID, 8)

# Plot their wOBA trajectories
batting_data %>%
  filter(playerID %in% sample_players) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=age, y=wOBA, color=playerID)) +
  geom_line(size=1.2) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title="Sample of Individual Career Trajectories in wOBA",
       subtitle="Randomly selected players with ≥10 seasons (≥200 PA each)",
       x="Age", y="wOBA", color="Player") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14)


```

```{r}
#GLOBAL AGE PERFORMANCE CURVE wOBA
m <- gam(wOBA ~ s(age, k=8), data=batting_data, weights=PA, method="REML")
newdat <- data.frame(age = seq(18,45,0.25))
pred <- predict(m, newdat, se.fit=TRUE)

curve_df <- data.frame(age=newdat$age,
                       wOBA=pred$fit,
                       lo=pred$fit-2*pred$se.fit,
                       hi=pred$fit+2*pred$se.fit)

ggplot(curve_df, aes(x=age,y=wOBA)) +
  geom_line(color="blue", size=1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=lo,ymax=hi), fill="blue", alpha=0.2) +
  labs(title="League-wide Age-Performance Curve (wOBA)",
       x="Age", y="wOBA")

```

```{r}
#PLAYERS OVER 40
old_players <- batting_data %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  filter(max(age) >= 40) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(old_players, aes(x=age,y=wOBA,group=playerID,color=playerID)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title="Players Who Played Past Age 40 (Career wOBA)",
       x="Age", y="wOBA")

```

```{r}
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(purrr)

#PLAYER SURVIVAL
batting_data <- batting_data %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  arrange(yearID) %>%
  mutate(next_year = lead(yearID),
         survived_next_year = ifelse(!is.na(next_year),
                                     1,
                                     ifelse(yearID==2024,
                                            1,
                                            0))) %>%
         
  ungroup()

head(batting_data %>% select(playerID, yearID, age, wOBA, survived_next_year))

#AVG RETIREMENT AGE
# Last season per player
retirement_ages <- batting_data %>%
  filter(survived_next_year == 0) %>%  # last observed season
  select(playerID, age)



#SURVIVAL PROPORTION
survival_proportions <- tibble(age_cut = c(35, 40, 45)) %>%
  mutate(proportion_surviving = map_dbl(age_cut, ~ mean(retirement_ages$age > .x)))

survival_proportions

#KM SURVIVAL ESTMATE
surv_data <- batting_data %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  summarise(
    start_age = min(age),
    end_age = max(age),
    retired = ifelse(survived_next_year[which.max(age)] == 0, 1, 0),  # 1 if retired at last season
    .groups="drop"
  )

# Kaplan-Meier fit
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(time = start_age, time2 = end_age, event = retired) ~ 1, data = surv_data)

# Summary
summary(km_fit)

# Plot KM curve
ggsurvplot(km_fit,
           conf.int = TRUE,
           xlab = "Age",
           ylab = "Probability of Still Playing",
           title = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve: Player Careers",
           risk.table = TRUE)

```

```{r}
library(Lahman)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)

# Prepare player-season dataset
batting_data <- Batting %>%
  left_join(People %>% select(playerID, nameFirst, nameLast, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay), by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_date = make_date(birthYear, pmax(1, birthMonth, na.rm=TRUE), pmax(1, birthDay, na.rm=TRUE)),
    age = year(make_date(yearID, 7, 1)) - year(birth_date),
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SH + SF,
    wOBA = (0.69*BB + 0.72*HBP + 0.89*H + 1.27*X2B + 1.62*X3B + 2.10*HR) / pmax(PA,1)
  ) %>%
  filter(PA >= 200, between(age, 18, 45))

# Identify longevity groups
player_max_age <- batting_data %>%
  group_by(playerID, nameFirst, nameLast) %>%
  summarise(max_age = max(age), .groups="drop")

# Players who lasted past 40
long_lifers <- player_max_age %>%
  filter(max_age >= 40) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = max_age, n = 20)

# Control players: long careers but retired before 40
controls <- player_max_age %>%
  filter(max_age < 40, max_age >= 36) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20)

# Combine player lists and tag group
selected_players <- bind_rows(
  long_lifers %>% mutate(group = "40+ Career"),
  controls %>% mutate(group = "Control")
)

# Filter batting_data for these players
plot_data <- batting_data %>%
  filter(playerID %in% selected_players$playerID) %>%
  left_join(selected_players %>% select(playerID, group), by="playerID") %>%
  mutate(player_name = paste(nameFirst, nameLast))

# Plot trajectories
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x=age, y=wOBA, color=group, group=player_name)) +
  geom_line(size=1) +
  geom_point(size=1.5, alpha=0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Career wOBA Trajectories: Longevity Players vs Controls",
    x = "Age",
    y = "wOBA",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")


summary_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(group, age) %>%
  summarise(
    wOBA_mean = mean(wOBA),
    wOBA_sd = sd(wOBA),
    .groups="drop"
  )

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x=age, y=wOBA_mean, color=group, fill=group)) +
  geom_line(size=1.5) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=wOBA_mean-wOBA_sd, ymax=wOBA_mean+wOBA_sd), alpha=0.2) +
  labs(title="Average Career wOBA with Variability",
       x="Age", y="wOBA", color="Group", fill="Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14)

```

COX MODEL W/OUT PREDICTORS EX
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

# Use only last observed age per player
survival_data_simple <- survival_data_model %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  summarise(
    age = max(age),
    lag_wOBA = last(lag_wOBA),
    lag_PA   = last(lag_PA),
    cum_PA   = last(cum_PA),
    event    = last(event),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Fit Cox model
cox_model_simple <- coxph(
  Surv(age, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + cum_PA,
  data = survival_data_simple
)

# Example player data
example_player <- data.frame(
  lag_wOBA = 0.350,
  lag_PA   = 500,
  cum_PA   = 3000
)

# Create survfit object for the example player
example_fit <- survfit(cox_model_simple, newdata = example_player)

# Base R plot
plot(
  example_fit,
  xlab = "Age",
  ylab = "Probability of Still Playing",
  main = "Cox PH Survival Prediction (Example Player)",
  conf.int = TRUE,   # show confidence intervals
  col = "blue",
  lwd = 2
)

# Add grid for readability
grid()


```



COX MODEL With Predictors
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(tibble)


# Survival curve for the *entire dataset* (baseline curve)
#overall_fit <- survfit(cox_model, newdata = survival_data_model, type = "aalen")
# Marginal survival curve (average over dataset covariates)
overall_fit <- survfit(cox_model, newdata = survival_data_model[1,])

plot(overall_fit,
     xlab = "Age",
     ylab = "Probability of Still Playing",
     main = "Cox PH Marginal Survival Curve (All Players)")


head(survival_data_model)
#view(survival_data)
view(survival_data_model)

```

```{r}
library(survival)
data(pbc)  # Primary biliary cirrhosis dataset

# Create baseline survival object
pbc_base <- pbc[, !names(pbc) %in% "bili"]
pbc2 <- tmerge(pbc_base, pbc, id=id, death=event(time, status))
pbc2 <- tmerge(pbc2, pbc, id=id, bili = tdc(time, bili))

# Add time-dependent covariate (serum bilirubin updated annually)
#pbc2 <- tmerge(pbc2, pbc, id=id, bili = tdc(time, bili))
pbc2$bili
# Fit Cox model with time-dependent covariate
fit <- coxph(Surv(tstart, tstop, death) ~ bili + age + sex, data=pbc2)
summary(fit)
view(pbc2)
pbc2$bili
```
```{r}
cox_model_cluster <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + cluster(playerID),
  data = survival_data_model
)
summary(cox_model_cluster)

cox_model_frailty <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + frailty(playerID),
  data = survival_data_model
)
summary(cox_model_frailty)

cox_model_strata <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + strata(playerID),
  data = survival_data_model
)

```

```{r}
library(coxme)
fit <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + frailty(playerID),
             data = survival_data_model)
fit
fit_no_frailty <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G,
                        data = survival_data_model)

surv_curves <- survfit(fit_no_frailty, newdata = survival_data_model)
expected_time <- sum(diff(surv_curves$time) * head(surv_curves$surv, -1))
fit_me <- coxme(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + (1 | playerID),
                data = survival_data_model)

frailty_estimates <- ranef(fit_me)   # works here`

```

```{r}
#predict(fit, type = "expected")
tail(surv_curves)
dim(survival_data_model)
# Kaplan-Meier fit
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(time = start_age, time2 = end_age, event = retired) ~ 1, data = surv_data)

# Summary
summary(km_fit)
fit_no_frailty <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G,
                        data = survival_data_model)

surv_curves <- survfit(fit_no_frailty, newdata = survival_data_model)
surv_curves
dim(surv_data)
```

```{r}
# Survival curve for the first row of data
new_player <- survival_data_model[1, , drop = FALSE]
surv_curves <- survfit(fit_no_frailty, newdata = new_player)

# Plot survival curve
plot(surv_curves,
     xlab = "Time",
     ylab = "Survival probability",
     main = "Predicted Survival Curve (No Frailty)",
     col = "blue", lwd = 2)

grid()

# Add expected survival time as a vertical line
abline(v = expected_time, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(expected_time, 0.5, labels = paste0("E[T] ≈ ", round(expected_time, 1)),
     pos = 4, col = "red")

surv_curves[3696]
```
```{r}
# Function to compute expected time from survfit object
expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj) {
  sum(diff(surv_obj$time) * head(surv_obj$surv, -1))
}

# Compute expected retirement for first 20 players
expected_times <- sapply(1:22, function(i) {
  s <- survfit(fit_no_frailty, newdata = survival_data_model %>%
                 filter(playerID == "aaronha01") %>%
                 slice(i))
    
  expected_survival_time(s)
})

# Plot
barplot(expected_times,
        las = 2,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = "Predicted Expected Retirement Age",
        ylab = "Age")
expected_times
```
LINEAR AGE PREICTION
```{r}
# --- Fit Cox model with age included ---
fit_no_frailty_age <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age,
                            data = survival_data_model)

# --- Helper: Expected survival time (truncate long tails beyond observed range) ---
expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj) {
  # Only integrate over time up to the 95th percentile of observed follow-up
  max_time <- quantile(surv_obj$time, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- surv_obj$time <= max_time
  sum(diff(surv_obj$time[idx]) * head(surv_obj$surv[idx], -1))
}

# --- Choose player to analyze (example: Hank Aaron) ---
player_id <- "aaronha01"

# --- Subset player data ---
player_data <- survival_data_model %>%
  filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
  arrange(start)

# --- Compute expected remaining career time for each season ---
expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
  s <- survfit(fit_no_frailty_age, newdata = player_data[i, ])
  
  # If the player is older than any observed player in training data,
  # scale down the expected remaining time proportionally
  max_age <- max(survival_data_model$age, na.rm = TRUE)
  age_factor <- max(0, 1 - (player_data$age[i] - 35) / (max_age - 35))  # after 35, taper predictions
  age_factor <- pmax(age_factor, 0.1)  # ensure nonzero but small value
  
  est <- expected_survival_time(s)
  est * age_factor  # apply age-based adjustment
})

# --- Compute predicted retirement age ---
predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining

# --- Combine into a data frame for plotting ---
results <- data.frame(
  season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
  current_age = player_data$age,
  expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
  predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age
)

# --- Plot predicted retirement age vs current age ---
plot(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age,
     type = "b", pch = 19, col = "skyblue",
     xlab = "Current Age",
     ylab = "Predicted Retirement Age",
     main = paste("Predicted Retirement Age vs Current Age for", player_id))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age + 0.3,
     labels = round(results$expected_remaining, 1), cex = 0.7)

# --- Histogram-style plot for expected years remaining ---
barplot(results$expected_remaining,
        names.arg = round(results$current_age, 0),
        las = 2,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
        xlab = "Current Age",
        ylab = "Expected Years Remaining")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

```

SPLINING AGE
```{r}
# --- Load required package ---
library(splines)   # for natural splines

# --- Fit Cox model with nonlinear (spline) effect for age ---
fit_no_frailty_age <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + ns(age, df = 3),
  data = survival_data_model
)

# --- Helper: Expected survival time (truncate long tails beyond observed range) ---
expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj) {
  # Only integrate over time up to the 95th percentile of observed follow-up
  max_time <- quantile(surv_obj$time, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- surv_obj$time <= max_time
  sum(diff(surv_obj$time[idx]) * head(surv_obj$surv[idx], -1))
}

# --- Choose player to analyze (example: Hank Aaron) ---
player_id <- "aaronha01"

# --- Subset player data ---
player_data <- survival_data_model %>%
  filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
  arrange(start)

# --- Compute expected remaining career time for each season ---
expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
  s <- survfit(fit_no_frailty_age, newdata = player_data[i, ])
  
  # Adjust predictions for extreme ages (beyond observed data range)
  max_age <- max(survival_data_model$age, na.rm = TRUE)
  min_age <- min(survival_data_model$age, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Smoothly taper expected remaining years after age 35 using spline-like decay
  age_factor <- max(0, 1 - (player_data$age[i] - 35) / (max_age - 35))
  age_factor <- pmax(age_factor, 0.1)  # keep at least 10% weight
  
  est <- expected_survival_time(s)
  est * age_factor
})

# --- Compute predicted retirement age ---
predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining

# --- Combine results for plotting ---
results <- data.frame(
  season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
  current_age = player_data$age,
  expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
  predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age
)

# --- Plot: Predicted retirement age vs current age ---
plot(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age,
     type = "b", pch = 19, col = "skyblue",
     xlab = "Current Age",
     ylab = "Predicted Retirement Age",
     main = paste("Predicted Retirement Age vs Current Age for", player_id))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age + 0.3,
     labels = round(results$expected_remaining, 1), cex = 0.7)

# --- Histogram-style plot for expected years remaining ---
barplot(results$expected_remaining,
        names.arg = round(results$current_age, 0),
        las = 2,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
        xlab = "Current Age",
        ylab = "Expected Years Remaining")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

```


```{r}
# --- Load required packages ---
library(dplyr)
library(splines)
library(survival)
library(zoo)

# ================================
# 1. PREPARE DATA
# ================================

# Assumes survival_data_model has:
# playerID, season, age, wOBA, PA, G, event (1=career end that year)

survival_data_model2 <- survival_data_model %>%
  arrange(playerID, yearID) %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  mutate(
    years_in_league = row_number() - 1,   # 0, 1, 2, ...
    start = years_in_league,
    stop  = years_in_league + 1
  ) %>%
  # lag and cumulative features
  mutate(
    lag_wOBA = lag(wOBA, 1),
    lag_PA   = lag(PA, 1),
    lag_G    = lag(G, 1),
    career_PA = cumsum(lag(PA, default = 0)),
    career_G  = cumsum(lag(G, default = 0)),
    #career woba per pa 
    career_wOBA = ifelse(career_PA > 0,
                         cumsum(lag(wOBA * PA, default = 0)) / pmax(career_PA, 1),
                         NA_real_),
    #career woba cumulatively
    #career_wOBA2 = ifelse(career_PA > 0,
     #                    cumsum(lag(wOBA * PA, default = 0)), NA_real_),
    wOBA_rolling3 = zoo::rollapply(lag(wOBA, 1), width = 3,
                                   FUN = mean, align = "right", fill = NA)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Drop first-season rows with missing lags
survival_data_model2 <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(lag_wOBA))

# ================================
# 2. FIT COX MODEL
# ================================

fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA,
  data = survival_data_model2
)
#career_wOBA + career_PA + 
summary(fit_cox)
cox.zph(fit_cox)  # check proportional hazards

# ================================
# 3. EXPECTED SURVIVAL TIME FUNCTION
# ================================

expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj, max_quantile = 0.95) {
  times <- surv_obj$time
  surv  <- surv_obj$surv
  if(length(times) < 2) return(0)
  max_time <- quantile(times, probs = max_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- which(times <= max_time)
  if(length(idx) < 2) return(0)
  times_sub <- times[idx]
  surv_sub  <- surv[idx]
  dt <- diff(c(0, times_sub))
  auc <- sum(surv_sub * dt)
  return(auc)
}

# ================================
# 4. PREDICT EXPECTED REMAINING SEASONS FOR ONE PLAYER
# ================================

player_id <- "aaronha01"  # example

player_data <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
  arrange(start)

expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
  newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
  sf <- survfit(fit_cox, newdata = newrow)
  est <- expected_survival_time(sf)
  
  # Optional taper for very old ages (outside observed range)
#   max_age <- max(survival_data_model2$age, na.rm = TRUE)
#   if (newrow$age > 40) {
#     age_factor <- max(0.1, 1 - (newrow$age - 40) / (max_age - 40))
#   } else {
#     age_factor <- 1
#   }
#   est * age_factor
 })

predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining

results <- data.frame(
  season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
  season = player_data$yearID,
  current_age = player_data$age,
  expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
  predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age
)

# ================================
# 5. PLOTS
# ================================

# --- Predicted retirement age vs. current age ---
plot(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age,
     type = "b", pch = 19, col = "skyblue",
     xlab = "Current Age",
     ylab = "Predicted Retirement Age",
     main = paste("Predicted Retirement Age vs Current Age for", player_id))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age + 0.3,
     labels = round(results$expected_remaining, 1), cex = 0.7)

# --- Expected years remaining (barplot) ---
barplot(results$expected_remaining,
        names.arg = round(results$current_age, 0),
        las = 2,
        col = "skyblue",
        main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
        xlab = "Current Age",
        ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

# ================================
# 6. OPTIONAL: Compare with another player
# ================================
# player_id2 <- "someother01"
# repeat the same prediction block above for comparison
fit_cox
```

```{r}
# ================================
# Cox model + nonlinear effect plot for career_PA
# ================================

library(survival)
library(splines)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# --- 1. Fit Cox model with spline for career_PA
fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + ns(career_PA, df = 3),
  data = survival_data_model2
)

# --- 2. Create prediction grid for career_PA
# Set other predictors to median values
median_vals <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  summarise(
    age = median(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_wOBA = median(lag_wOBA, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_PA = median(lag_PA, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_G = median(lag_G, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

career_PA_grid <- seq(
  min(survival_data_model2$career_PA, na.rm = TRUE),
  max(survival_data_model2$career_PA, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 100
)

newdata <- data.frame(
  age = median_vals$age,
  lag_wOBA = median_vals$lag_wOBA,
  lag_PA = median_vals$lag_PA,
  lag_G = median_vals$lag_G,
  career_PA = career_PA_grid
)

# --- 3. Predict linear predictor and compute hazard ratios
linear_pred <- predict(fit_cox, newdata = newdata, type = "lp", se.fit = TRUE)

hr <- exp(linear_pred$fit)
hr_upper <- exp(linear_pred$fit + 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)
hr_lower <- exp(linear_pred$fit - 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  career_PA = career_PA_grid,
  HR = hr,
  HR_upper = hr_upper,
  HR_lower = hr_lower
)

# --- 4. Plot nonlinear effect of career_PA
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = career_PA, y = HR)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = HR_lower, ymax = HR_upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "blue") +
  labs(
    x = "Career Plate Appearances",
    y = "Hazard Ratio",
    title = "Nonlinear Effect of Career PA on Hazard of Retirement"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
library(survival)
library(splines)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
survival_data_model2 <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  filter(yearID != 2020)
# --- 1. Fit Cox model with spline for lag_G instead of career_PA
fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(lag_G, df = 3),
    #ns(age, df = 3) + 
    #lag_wOBA + lag_PA + ns(lag_G, df = 3) + career_PA,
  data = survival_data_model2
)

# --- 2. Create prediction grid for lag_G
# Set other predictors to median values
median_vals <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  summarise(
    age = median(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_wOBA = median(lag_wOBA, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_PA = median(lag_PA, na.rm = TRUE),
    career_PA = median(career_PA, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

lag_G_grid <- seq(
  min(survival_data_model2$lag_G, na.rm = TRUE),
  max(survival_data_model2$lag_G, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 100
)

newdata <- data.frame(
  age = median_vals$age,
  lag_wOBA = median_vals$lag_wOBA,
  lag_PA = median_vals$lag_PA,
  lag_G = lag_G_grid,
  career_PA = median_vals$career_PA
)

# --- 3. Predict linear predictor and compute hazard ratios
linear_pred <- predict(fit_cox, newdata = newdata, type = "lp", se.fit = TRUE)

hr <- exp(linear_pred$fit)
hr_upper <- exp(linear_pred$fit + 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)
hr_lower <- exp(linear_pred$fit - 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  lag_G = lag_G_grid,
  HR = hr,
  HR_upper = hr_upper,
  HR_lower = hr_lower
)

# --- 4. Plot nonlinear effect of lag_G
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = lag_G, y = HR)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred", size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = HR_lower, ymax = HR_upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkred") +
  labs(
    x = "Games Played in Previous Season (lag_G)",
    y = "Hazard Ratio",
    title = "Nonlinear Effect of lag_G on Hazard of Retirement"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# ================================
# Cox model + nonlinear effect plot for age
# ================================

library(survival)
library(splines)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# --- 1. Fit Cox model with spline for age
fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + ns(career_PA, df = 3),
  data = survival_data_model2
)

# --- 2. Create prediction grid for age
# Set other predictors to median values
median_vals <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  summarise(
    lag_wOBA = median(lag_wOBA, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_PA = median(lag_PA, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_G = median(lag_G, na.rm = TRUE),
    career_PA = median(career_PA, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

age_grid <- seq(
  min(survival_data_model2$age, na.rm = TRUE),
  max(survival_data_model2$age, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 100
)

newdata <- data.frame(
  age = age_grid,
  lag_wOBA = median_vals$lag_wOBA,
  lag_PA = median_vals$lag_PA,
  lag_G = median_vals$lag_G,
  career_PA = median_vals$career_PA
)

# --- 3. Predict linear predictor and compute hazard ratios
linear_pred <- predict(fit_cox, newdata = newdata, type = "lp", se.fit = TRUE)

hr <- exp(linear_pred$fit)
hr_upper <- exp(linear_pred$fit + 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)
hr_lower <- exp(linear_pred$fit - 1.96 * linear_pred$se.fit)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  age = age_grid,
  HR = hr,
  HR_upper = hr_upper,
  HR_lower = hr_lower
)

# --- 4. Plot nonlinear effect of age
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = age, y = HR)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = HR_lower, ymax = HR_upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkgreen") +
  labs(
    x = "Age",
    y = "Hazard Ratio",
    title = "Nonlinear Effect of Age on Hazard of Retirement"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```



Fit comparison
```{r}
#Linear Baseline
fit_linear <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ age + lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G, data = survival_data_model2)
#Spline
fit_spline <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ ns(age,3) + lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G, data = survival_data_model2)
#interaction
fit_interact <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ ns(age,3) + lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA, data = survival_data_model2 %>%
                        mutate(age = scale(age, scale = FALSE),
                               career_PA = scale(career_PA, scale = FALSE)))
#Spline + Interaction
fit_complex <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ ns(age,3)*career_PA + lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G, data = survival_data_model2)

fit_two_nonlinear <- fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + ns(career_PA, df = 3),
  data = survival_data_model2
)

fit_interact_nonlinear <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + ns(age, df=3)*ns(career_PA, df=3), data = survival_data_model2)

fit_linear_with_inter <- coxph(formula = Surv(start, stop, event) ~ lag_wOBA + 
    lag_PA + lag_G + age * career_PA, data = survival_data_model2 %>% 
    mutate(age = scale(age, scale = FALSE), career_PA = scale(career_PA, 
        scale = FALSE)))

fit_Inter_double <- coxph(Surv(start, stop, event) ~ ns(age,3) + ns(career_PA, 3) + lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA, data = survival_data_model2 %>%
                        mutate(age = scale(age, scale = FALSE),
                               career_PA = scale(career_PA, scale = FALSE)))

AIC(fit_linear, fit_spline, fit_interact, fit_complex, fit_two_nonlinear, fit_interact_nonlinear, fit_linear_with_inter, fit_Inter_double)
BIC(fit_linear, fit_spline, fit_interact, fit_complex, fit_two_nonlinear, fit_interact_nonlinear, fit_linear_with_inter, fit_Inter_double)
anova(fit_interact, fit_linear_with_inter)

summary(fit_cox)$concordance
```
PLAYER TRAJECTORY PLOTS:
```{r}
# --- Load required packages ---
library(dplyr)
library(splines)
library(survival)
library(zoo)

# ================================
# 1. PREPARE DATA
# ================================

# Assumes survival_data_model has:
# playerID, season, age, wOBA, PA, G, event (1=career end that year)

survival_data_model2 <- survival_data_model %>%
  arrange(playerID, yearID) %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  mutate(
    years_in_league = row_number() - 1,   # 0, 1, 2, ...
    start = years_in_league,
    stop  = years_in_league + 1
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    #lag_wOBA = lag(wOBA, 1),
    #lag_PA   = lag(PA, 1),
    #lag_G    = lag(G, 1),
    career_PA = cumsum(lag(PA, default = 0)),
    career_G  = cumsum(lag(G, default = 0)),
    career_wOBA = ifelse(career_PA > 0,
                         cumsum(lag(wOBA * PA, default = 0)) / pmax(career_PA, 1),
                         NA_real_),
    wOBA_rolling3 = zoo::rollapply(lag(wOBA, 1), width = 3,
                                   FUN = mean, align = "right", fill = NA)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(lag_wOBA))

# ================================
# 2. FIT COX MODEL
# ================================

fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA,
  data = survival_data_model2
)

summary(fit_cox)
cox.zph(fit_cox)  # check proportional hazards

# ================================
# 3. EXPECTED SURVIVAL TIME FUNCTION
# ================================

expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj, max_quantile = 0.95) {
  times <- surv_obj$time
  surv  <- surv_obj$surv
  if(length(times) < 2) return(0)
  max_time <- quantile(times, probs = max_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- which(times <= max_time)
  if(length(idx) < 2) return(0)
  times_sub <- times[idx]
  surv_sub  <- surv[idx]
  dt <- diff(c(0, times_sub))
  auc <- sum(surv_sub * dt)
  return(auc)
}

# ================================
# 4. PREDICT FOR MULTIPLE PLAYERS
# ================================

players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01", "forsylo01")  
player_id = "troutmi01"
for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  cat("\n\n====================\n")
  cat("Generating plots for:", player_id, "\n")
  cat("====================\n")
  
  player_data <- survival_data_model2 %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start) %>%
    slice(-1)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data found for", player_id, "\n")
    next
  }
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- survfit(fit_cox, newdata = newrow)
    est <- expected_survival_time(sf)
    est
  })
  
  predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
    season = player_data$yearID,
    current_age = player_data$age,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age
  )
  
  # ================================
  # 5. PLOTS (same as your code)
  # ================================
  
  # --- Predicted retirement age vs. current age ---
  plot(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age,
       type = "b", pch = 19, col = "skyblue",
       xlab = "Current Age",
       ylab = "Predicted Retirement Age",
       main = paste("Predicted Retirement Age vs Current Age for", player_id))
  abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
  text(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age + 0.3,
       labels = round(results$expected_remaining, 1), cex = 0.7)
  
  # --- Expected years remaining (barplot) ---
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = round(results$current_age, 0),
          las = 2,
          col = "skyblue",
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
          xlab = "Current Age",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
}


```

Gap Year 0 Assignment Option
```{r}
# ================================
# 1. FILL MISSING YEARS AND REBUILD FEATURES
# ================================

full_years <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  tidyr::complete(yearID = full_seq(yearID, 1)) %>%
  fill(age, .direction = "downup") %>%
  mutate(
    G = replace_na(G, 0),
    PA = replace_na(PA, 0),
    wOBA = ifelse(is.na(wOBA), 0, wOBA)
  ) %>%
  arrange(playerID, yearID) %>%
  # rebuild survival timing variables
  mutate(
    years_in_league = row_number() - 1,
    start = years_in_league,
    stop = years_in_league + 1,
    # assume career continues until last observed year (event missing)
    event = ifelse(yearID == max(yearID, na.rm = TRUE), event[!is.na(event)][1], 0)
  ) %>%
  # rebuild lag and cumulative variables
  mutate(
    lag_wOBA = lag(wOBA, 1),
    lag_PA   = lag(PA, 1),
    lag_G    = lag(G, 1),
    career_PA = cumsum(lag(PA, default = 0)),
    career_G  = cumsum(lag(G, default = 0)),
    career_wOBA = ifelse(career_PA > 0,
                         cumsum(lag(wOBA * PA, default = 0)) / pmax(career_PA, 1),
                         NA_real_),
    wOBA_rolling3 = zoo::rollapply(lag(wOBA, 1), width = 3,
                                   FUN = mean, align = "right", fill = NA)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# drop first rows with missing lags
full_years <- full_years %>%
  filter(!is.na(lag_wOBA))

# ================================
# 2. FIT COX MODEL
# ================================

fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) + 
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age * career_PA,
  data = full_years
)

summary(fit_cox)
cox.zph(fit_cox, transform = "km")

# ================================
# 3. EXPECTED SURVIVAL TIME FUNCTION
# ================================

expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj, max_quantile = 0.95) {
  times <- surv_obj$time
  surv  <- surv_obj$surv
  if (length(times) < 2) return(0)
  max_time <- quantile(times, probs = max_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- which(times <= max_time)
  if (length(idx) < 2) return(0)
  times_sub <- times[idx]
  surv_sub  <- surv[idx]
  dt <- diff(c(0, times_sub))
  auc <- sum(surv_sub * dt)
  return(auc)
}

# ================================
# 4. PREDICT FOR MULTIPLE PLAYERS
# ================================

players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01", "forsylo01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  cat("\n\n====================\n")
  cat("Generating plots for:", player_id, "\n")
  cat("====================\n")
  
  player_data <- full_years %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data found for", player_id, "\n")
    next
  }
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- survfit(fit_cox, newdata = newrow)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
    season = player_data$yearID,
    current_age = player_data$age,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age
  )
  
  # --- Predicted retirement age vs. current age ---
  plot(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age,
       type = "b", pch = 19, col = "skyblue",
       xlab = "Current Age",
       ylab = "Predicted Retirement Age",
       main = paste("Predicted Retirement Age vs Current Age for", player_id))
  abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
  text(results$current_age, results$predicted_retirement_age + 0.3,
       labels = round(results$expected_remaining, 1), cex = 0.7)
  
  # --- Expected years remaining (barplot) ---
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = round(results$current_age, 0),
          las = 2,
          col = "skyblue",
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
          xlab = "Current Age",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
}

```

Reformatted data
```{r}

Batting <- Lahman::Batting
People <- Lahman::People
# ================================
# 0. ENHANCE RAW BATTING DATA — KEEP ALL ROWS
# ================================
# --- Join People info and compute age for each season ---
Batting <- Batting %>%
  left_join(
    People %>%
      select(playerID, birthYear, birthMonth, birthDay, nameFirst, nameLast, bats, throws),
    by = "playerID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Estimate player age for that season
    # (approximate, since we don’t have exact game date — assumes mid-season)
    age = yearID - birthYear - ifelse(birthMonth > 7, 1, 0)
  )
# ---- Compute extra metrics ----
Batting <- Batting %>%
  mutate(
    # Plate Appearances
    PA = AB + BB + HBP + SF + SH,
    
    # Singles (1B)
    X1B = H - X2B - X3B - HR,
    
    # Weighted On-Base Average (wOBA)
    wOBA = (
      (0.69 * (BB - ifelse(is.na(IBB), 0, IBB))) +
      (0.72 * HBP) +
      (0.89 * X1B) +
      (1.27 * X2B) +
      (1.62 * X3B) +
      (2.10 * HR)
    ) / (AB + BB - ifelse(is.na(IBB), 0, IBB) + SF + HBP)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    wOBA = ifelse(is.nan(wOBA) | is.infinite(wOBA), NA, wOBA)
  )

# ---- Build survival model structure ----
survival_data_model2 <- Batting %>%
  arrange(playerID, yearID) %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  mutate(
    years_in_league = row_number() - 1,  # start counting at 0
    start = years_in_league,
    stop  = years_in_league + 1,
    
    # Lag and cumulative stats
    lag_wOBA = lag(wOBA, 1),
    lag_PA   = lag(PA, 1),
    lag_G    = lag(G, 1),
    
    career_PA = cumsum(lag(PA, default = 0)),
    career_G  = cumsum(lag(G, default = 0)),

    # Flag final available year
    event = if_else(row_number() == n(), 1, 0),
    
    # Flag gap years
    missed_prev_year = if_else(yearID - lag(yearID, default = first(yearID)) > 1, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# # Replace NA wOBA with 0 only for modeling stability
# survival_data_model2 <- survival_data_model2 %>%
#   mutate(
#     wOBA = ifelse(is.na(wOBA), 0, wOBA)
#   )

glimpse(survival_data_model2)
saveRDS(survival_data_model2, "survival_data_model2.rds")

```


Absence Flagging 
```{r}
# ================================
# 1. FIT COX MODEL (same as before)
# ================================

fit_cox <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) +
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA,
  data = survival_data_model2
)

summary(fit_cox)

# ================================
# 2. HELPER FUNCTION
# ================================
expected_survival_time <- function(surv_obj, max_quantile = 0.95) {
  times <- surv_obj$time
  surv  <- surv_obj$surv
  if(length(times) < 2) return(0)
  max_time <- quantile(times, probs = max_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)
  idx <- which(times <= max_time)
  if(length(idx) < 2) return(0)
  times_sub <- times[idx]
  surv_sub  <- surv[idx]
  dt <- diff(c(0, times_sub))
  auc <- sum(surv_sub * dt)
  return(auc)
}

# ================================
# 3. PREDICT AND PLOT (with NA-safe handling)
# ================================

players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  cat("\n\n====================\n")
  cat("Generating plots for:", player_id, "\n")
  cat("====================\n")
  
  player_data <- survival_data_model2 %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) {
    cat("No data found for", player_id, "\n")
    next
  }

  # --- Replace NA predictors with safe defaults ---
  # player_data <- player_data %>%
  #   mutate(
  #     lag_wOBA = ifelse(is.na(lag_wOBA), 0, lag_wOBA),
  #     lag_PA   = ifelse(is.na(lag_PA), 0, lag_PA),
  #     lag_G    = ifelse(is.na(lag_G), 0, lag_G),
  #     career_PA = ifelse(is.na(career_PA), 0, career_PA),
  #     missed_prev_year = ifelse(is.na(missed_prev_year), 0, missed_prev_year)
  #   )
  # 
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- tryCatch(
      survfit(fit_cox, newdata = newrow),
      error = function(e) return(NULL)
    )
    if (is.null(sf)) return(0)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$yearID + expected_remaining
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
    season = player_data$yearID,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age,
    missed_prev_year = player_data$missed_prev_year
  )
  
  # --- PLOTS ---
  bar_cols <- ifelse(results$missed_prev_year == 1, "tomato", "skyblue")
  
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = results$season,
          las = 2,
          col = bar_cols,
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
          xlab = "Season (Year)",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
  
  legend("topright", legend = c("Normal Season", "Missed Prior Year"),
         fill = c("skyblue", "tomato"), bty = "n")
}

```
REMOVING YEARS WITHOUT LAGS
```{r}
# ===========================================
# MODEL 1: Remove Years Without Lag Variables
# ===========================================

# Drop any rows with missing lag_wOBA, lag_PA, or lag_G
survival_data_noNA <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(lag_wOBA) & !is.na(lag_PA) & !is.na(lag_G))

# Fit Cox model
fit_cox_noNA <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) +
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age * career_PA,
  data = survival_data_noNA
)

# Plot predictions for selected players
players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  player_data <- survival_data_noNA %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) next
  
  # Handle any remaining NAs safely
  player_data <- player_data %>%
    mutate(across(c(lag_wOBA, lag_PA, lag_G, career_PA, missed_prev_year),
                  ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)))
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- tryCatch(survfit(fit_cox_noNA, newdata = newrow), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(sf)) return(0)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season = player_data$yearID,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    missed_prev_year = player_data$missed_prev_year
  )
  
  bar_cols <- ifelse(results$missed_prev_year == 1, "tomato", "skyblue")
  
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = results$season,
          las = 2,
          col = bar_cols,
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career (Removed Lags) —", player_id),
          xlab = "Season",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
  legend("topright", legend = c("Normal Season", "Missed Prior Year"),
         fill = c("skyblue", "tomato"), bty = "n")
}

```

IMPUTING MISSING LAGS
```{r}
# ===========================================
# MODEL 2: Impute Missing Lag Variables
# ===========================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# For each player, fill NA lag values with the most recent non-missing lag
survival_data_imputed <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  arrange(playerID, yearID) %>%
  group_by(playerID) %>%
  fill(lag_wOBA, .direction = "down") %>%
  fill(lag_PA,   .direction = "down") %>%
  fill(lag_G,    .direction = "down") %>%
  ungroup()

# Fit Cox model with interactions
fit_cox_imputed <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) +
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age * career_PA +
    lag_G * missed_prev_year + 
    lag_wOBA * missed_prev_year,
  data = survival_data_imputed
)

# Plot predictions for selected players
players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  player_data <- survival_data_imputed %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) next
  
  # Replace any remaining NA with 0 (rare, only if first career year is missing)
  player_data <- player_data %>%
    mutate(across(c(lag_wOBA, lag_PA, lag_G, career_PA, missed_prev_year),
                  ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)))
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- tryCatch(survfit(fit_cox_imputed, newdata = newrow), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(sf)) return(0)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season = player_data$yearID,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    missed_prev_year = player_data$missed_prev_year
  )
  
  bar_cols <- ifelse(results$missed_prev_year == 1, "tomato", "skyblue")
  
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = results$season,
          las = 2,
          col = bar_cols,
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career (Imputed Lags: Player-Specific) —", player_id),
          xlab = "Season",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
  legend("topright", legend = c("Normal Season", "Missed Prior Year"),
         fill = c("skyblue", "tomato"), bty = "n")
}

```


DROP 2020 SEASON
```{r}
# ================================
# 0. DROP 2020 SEASON
# ================================
survival_data_no_covid <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  filter(yearID != 2020)

# ================================
# 1. FIT COX MODEL
# ================================
fit_cox_no_covid <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) +
    lag_wOBA + lag_PA + lag_G + age*career_PA,
  data = survival_data_no_covid
)

summary(fit_cox_no_covid)

# ================================
# 2. PREDICT AND PLOT
# ================================
players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  player_data <- survival_data_no_covid %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) next
  
  # NA-safe replacement
  player_data <- player_data %>%
    mutate(
      lag_wOBA = ifelse(is.na(lag_wOBA), 0, lag_wOBA),
      lag_PA   = ifelse(is.na(lag_PA), 0, lag_PA),
      lag_G    = ifelse(is.na(lag_G), 0, lag_G),
      career_PA = ifelse(is.na(career_PA), 0, career_PA),
      missed_prev_year = ifelse(is.na(missed_prev_year), 0, missed_prev_year)
    )
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- tryCatch(survfit(fit_cox_no_covid, newdata = newrow), error = function(e) return(NULL))
    if (is.null(sf)) return(0)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
    season = player_data$yearID,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age,
    missed_prev_year = player_data$missed_prev_year
  )
  
  # Plot
  bar_cols <- ifelse(results$missed_prev_year == 1, "tomato", "skyblue")
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = results$season,
          las = 2,
          col = bar_cols,
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
          xlab = "Season (Year)",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
  legend("topright", legend = c("Normal Season", "Missed Prior Year"),
         fill = c("skyblue", "tomato"), bty = "n")
}

```

SCALING 2020 STATS TO FULL SEASON
```{r}
# ================================
# 0. CREATE COVID-ADJUSTED STATS
# ================================
covid_scaled_data <- survival_data_model2 %>%
  mutate(
    # Scale only 2020 season
    PA_adj = ifelse(yearID == 2021, lag_PA / 60 * 162, lag_PA),
    G_adj  = ifelse(yearID == 2021, lag_G  / 60 * 162, lag_G),
    wOBA_adj = lag_wOBA  # wOBA usually already rate, can leave as-is
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Recompute career totals using adjusted values
    career_PA_adj = cumsum(ifelse(is.na(PA_adj), 0, PA_adj)),
    career_G_adj  = cumsum(ifelse(is.na(G_adj), 0, G_adj))
  )

# ================================
# 1. FIT COX MODEL USING ADJUSTED STATS
# ================================
fit_cox_covid_scaled <- coxph(
  Surv(start, stop, event) ~ 
    ns(age, df = 3) +
    wOBA_adj + PA_adj + G_adj + age*career_PA_adj,
  data = covid_scaled_data
)

summary(fit_cox_covid_scaled)

# ================================
# 2. PREDICT AND PLOT
# ================================
players_to_plot <- c("aaronha01", "mayswi01", "troutmi01", "alonspe01")

for (player_id in players_to_plot) {
  
  player_data <- covid_scaled_data %>%
    filter(playerID == player_id) %>%
    arrange(start)
  
  if (nrow(player_data) == 0) next
  
  # NA-safe replacement
  player_data <- player_data %>%
    mutate(
      wOBA_adj = ifelse(is.na(wOBA_adj), 0, wOBA_adj),
      PA_adj   = ifelse(is.na(PA_adj), 0, PA_adj),
      G_adj    = ifelse(is.na(G_adj), 0, G_adj),
      career_PA_adj = ifelse(is.na(career_PA_adj), 0, career_PA_adj),
      missed_prev_year = ifelse(is.na(missed_prev_year), 0, missed_prev_year)
    )
  
  expected_remaining <- sapply(1:nrow(player_data), function(i) {
    newrow <- player_data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    sf <- tryCatch(survfit(fit_cox_covid_scaled, newdata = newrow), error = function(e) return(NULL))
    if (is.null(sf)) return(0)
    expected_survival_time(sf)
  })
  
  predicted_retirement_age <- player_data$age + expected_remaining
  
  results <- data.frame(
    season_index = 1:nrow(player_data),
    season = player_data$yearID,
    expected_remaining = expected_remaining,
    predicted_retirement_age = predicted_retirement_age,
    missed_prev_year = player_data$missed_prev_year
  )
  
  # Plot
  bar_cols <- ifelse(results$missed_prev_year == 1, "tomato", "skyblue")
  barplot(results$expected_remaining,
          names.arg = results$season,
          las = 2,
          col = bar_cols,
          main = paste("Predicted Remaining Career Length by Season for", player_id),
          xlab = "Season (Year)",
          ylab = "Expected Seasons Remaining")
  abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)
  legend("topright", legend = c("Normal Season", "Missed Prior Year"),
         fill = c("skyblue", "tomato"), bty = "n")
}

```
